question: The price of petrol shot up by 5%. Before the hike, the price was Rs. 82 per litre. A man travels 3045 km every month and his car gives a mileage of 15 km per litre. What is the increase in the monthly expenditure (to the nearest Rs.) on the man's travel due to the hike in the petrol prices?
Evaluate: 12 ( )% of 560 km + 66 ( )% of 240 km

In a city, the current number of literate people is 25,000. If the number of literate males decreases by 5% and the number of literate females increases by 10%, the total literate population becomes 25,250. What is the present number of literate males in the city?

In an election, 80% of eligible voters cast their votes. Of these votes, 5% were declared invalid. If a winning candidate received 11,400 votes, which accounted for 60% of the total valid votes, what was the total number of eligible voters enrolled in that election?
A tank contains 3,500 milliliters of water. If its full capacity is 0.005 kiloliters, what percentage of the tank is empty?
A bag contains 25% red balls, 35% green balls and the rest blue balls. If there are 40 blue balls, what is the total number of balls?

A school organized a field trip for 100 students and 10 chaperones. Each student was given a snack pack containing items equal to 15% of the total number of students. Each chaperone received a snack pack with items equal to 25% of the total number of students. How many snack items were distributed in total?
If 45% of a number is 0.9 more than 30% of it, what is the number?

In a competitive exam with 120 questions, there are three sections: General Knowledge (20 questions), Reasoning (50 questions), and English (50 questions). A candidate answered 70% of General Knowledge, 50% of Reasoning, and 30% of English questions correctly. If the minimum passing score is 60%, how many more questions did the candidate need to answer correctly to pass?
Seventy-five percent of a number is 15 more than two-thirds of that number. Find the number.
If 15% of a is equal to 25% of b, then find the ratio a : b.
If 20% of (P + Q) = 50% of (P – Q), then find the ratio P: Q.
If A is 25% more than B, and B is 20% more than C, then what is A : C?
If 25% of (P + Q) = 75% of (P - Q), then find the ratio P: Q.
If 30% of m is equal to 18% of n, what is the ratio m: n?
If 40% of A = 0.5 of B = of C, then what is A: B: C?
If 25% of A = 20% of B = 10% of C, find A : B : C.
If 12.5% of p is equal to 50% of q, what is the ratio p : q?
A student's marks in Mathematics were 20% more than in Physics. If he scored 72 in Physics, how many marks did he score in Mathematics?

An employee contributes 20% of their salary to charity. From the remaining amount, 40% is allocated to investments. The rest is divided for education and travel in the ratio 7: 5. If the expense on travel is Rs6000, what is the employee's total salary?
A man spends 80% of his income. His income has increased by 25%, but his expenditure remains the same. By what percent have his savings increased?
Three numbers are such that the second is 150% of the first, and the third is 80% of the second. What is the ratio of the first to the third?
A number was mistakenly increased by 25% instead of decreased by 20%. By what percent is the final result more than the CORRECT value?
The population of a village increases by 8% annually. If the current population is 58,320, what was it two years ago (approx)?
The difference between 20% of a number and 10% of the same number is 120. What is the number?
The value of a machine depreciates 10% in the first year, 20% in the second year, and 30% in the third year. If the initial value was Rs. 10,000, what is its value at the end of 3 years?
Out of her total monthly income, a woman spends 60% on household expenses and 15% on savings. The remaining is spent on other items. What percentage of her income is spent on these other items?
A's salary is 40% more than B's. If B's salary increases by 20% and A's increases by x%, then A's new salary becomes 25% more than B's new salary. What is the value of x?
A value is increased by 30% and then decreased by an unknown percentage to return to the original value. What is the percentage decrease required?
A student scored 80% in one subject and 60% in another. Both subjects have equal maximum marks. What is the overall percentage?
The price of a product is decreased by x% and then increased by x%. The final price becomes 10% less than the original. What is the value of x (approximately)?
The price of a product is decreased by x% and then increased by x%. The final price becomes 10% less than the original. What is the value of x (approximately)?
A bag contains 20% red balls, 30% green balls and the rest blue balls. If there are 80 blue balls, what is the total number of balls?

A candidate attempted 75% of the questions in an exam. He got 80% of the attempted ones correct, and 10% of the unattempted ones were also awarded marks due to grace. If the total number of questions is 200, how many did he get right?
A man spends 75% of his income. Due to inflation, his expenses increase by 20%, and his income increases by 10%. What is the percentage change in his savings?
A's salary is 40% more than B's. If B's salary increases by 20% and A's increases by x%, then A's new salary becomes 25% more than B's new salary. What is the value of x?
A man spends 70% of his income. His income increases by 10% and his expenditure by 5%. What is the percentage increase in his savings?
An investment is made in a business. In the first year, it gains 25%, in the second year, it loses 20%, and in the third year, it gains 10%. What is the overall gain or loss percentage?
Due to an error, a shopkeeper increases the selling price of a Rs 500 item by 10% instead of decreasing it by 10%. What is the percentage increase in the price due to this error?
The difference between 20% of a number and 10% of the same number is 120. What is the number?
A reduction of 20% in the price of sugar enables a person to buy 5 kg more for Rs. 200. What is the original price per kg?
The production of bottles rose to 26000 from 16000 in 2 years. Find the rate of growth per annum.
A man spends 30% of his salary on rent, 20% on groceries, and 25% on other expenses. If he saves Rs. 9,000, what is his salary?

The price of a bike increases by 20% in the first year and then decreases by 10% in the second year. If its final price becomes 59,400, find the original price of the bike. [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 3]
The population of a city increases by 25% in the first year and decreases by 20% in the second year. What is the net percentage change after 2 years? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 13 Nov Shift 1]

The population of a town increases by 20% in the first year, decreases by 10% in the second year, and again increases by 25% in the third year. If the population at the beginning was 40,000, what will it be after 3 years? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]
Which of the following is the least?

The population of a town increases by 20% in the first year, decreases by 10% in the second year, and again increases by 25% in the third year. If the population at the beginning was 40,000, what will it be after 3 years? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]
If the price of a notebook decreases by 25%, then a man can buy 5 more notebooks for Rs. 200. What is the new price (in Rs) of each notebook?
A student scored 20% more marks in Math than in Science. If the average of his marks in both subjects is 66, what are his marks in Science? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-1]

A school conducted a scholarship test where 60% of the students passed in English, 70% passed in Math, and 45% passed in both. What is the percentage of students who passed in at least one subject? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-1]

In a survey conducted at a university, it was found that 55% of the students like Mathematics, 45% like Physics, and 30% like both Mathematics and Physics. Among the remaining students, 25% prefer Computer Science, while the rest have no preference. If the total number of students surveyed is 800, how many students had no preference for any of the three subjects?

A person invests 35% of his income in mutual funds, 25% in fixed deposits, and the rest in recurring deposits. After 2 years, the mutual funds grow by 20%, fixed deposits by 10%, and recurring deposits by 15%. If his total income is Rs1,60,000, what is the total percentage return on his investment portfolio after 2 years?

Deepak spends 75% of his income, whereas Karan spends 80% of his income. Both of them save Rs. 7,500 each. The income of Sanjay is one-third of the sum of the income of Deepak and Karan. If Sanjay spends (7/9) th of his income, then find his savings.
A population falls by 12% in the first year. By what percentage must it increase in the second year so that by the end of the second year the population is exactly the same as the original?

In a cricket series, Team A had a winning rate of 70% after playing x matches. Then they lost 20 matches consecutively, and their winning rate fell to 50%. Find the total matches played by Team A before the losing streak.
If A is 50% more than B, and C is 30% less than the sum of A and B, then C is what percent of A?
A price drops from Rs. 200 to Rs. 150. What is the percentage decrease?
A's income is 40% less than B's. By what percent is B's income more than A's?

After two successive increments, a person's salary became 156.25% of the original salary. If the percentage increase in the second increment was equal to that in the first increment, then what was the percentage of each increment?
A town has a present population of 2,40,000. Every year the population increases by a birth rate of 12% and decreases by a death rate of 7%. Find the population of the town at the end of 3 years.
A student scored 72 marks in a test. If this was 60% of the total, what were the maximum marks?
A value increases from 400 to 500. What is the percentage increase?
A number is increased by 40% and then decreased by 30%. What is the net percentage change?
If A's income is 20% more than B's and B earns Rs. 50,000, what percent less is B's income than A's?
Sum of A and B is 300 percent more than the sum of C and D. C is 50 percent more than D. If A is 200 percent more than B and D = 200, then what is the value of A?
A number is first increased by 20% and then decreased by 20%. What is the overall percentage change in the number?
A number is first increased by 30% and then decreased by 30%. What is the net percentage change?
Sum of A and B is 100 percent more than the sum of C and D. C is 20 percent more than D. If A is 200 percent more than B and D = 500, then what is the value of A?
Sum of A and B is 150 percent more than the sum of C and D. C is 25 percent more than D. If A is 100 percent more than B and D = 400, then what is the value of A?
If 25% of a number is 80, what is 40% of that number?
The price of a product was increased by 10% and then decreased by 5%. What is the final price if the original price was Rs200?
A is 20 percent more than B. B is 30 percent more than C. C is 10 percent less than D. D is 30 percent less than E. By how much percentage is A more/less than E?
A shopkeeper increases the price of a product by 10%. If the original price was Rs. 500, what is the new price?
If 60% of a number is 48, what is the number?
What percent of Rs. 300 is Rs. 45?
What percent of 240 is 72?
20% of a number is 34. What is 50% of that number?

Out of 200 students, 20% failed the exam. Out of the remaining 20% passed the exam with distinction. Find the ratio of the students who passed the exam with distinction to the students who passed the exam without distinction.
If the price of a product increases by 25%, by what percent should the consumption be reduced to keep the expenditure same?

In an election, 25% voters did not vote. Out of the votes polled, 20% were declared invalid. The winner secured 60% of the valid votes and defeated his rival by 2,400 votes. Find the total number of voters.
Income of A is 20 percent more than income of B and the income of B is 25 percent more than income of C. A’s income is how much percentage more than C’s income?
If the length of a rectangular field is increased by 25%, then by what percentage should its breadth be reduced so that the area of the field remains constant?

A contractor estimated that a project would cost Rs5,00,000. He planned for 60% of the cost to go to materials, 25% to labor, and the rest as contingency. However, due to delays, material cost increased by 12.5%, labor cost increased by 10%, and contingency remained unchanged. What is the new total cost?

A bacteria culture increases by 20% every hour for 3 hours, then decreases by 10% in the 4th hour, and finally increases by 25% in the 5th hour. If the initial count was 5,000, what will be the approximate count after 5 hours?
The price of a shirt is increased by 20% and then again increased by 10%. What is the net percentage increase in price? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 2]
A student scores 30% marks and fails by 12 marks, while another student scores 44% marks and gets 16 marks more than the pass marks. What is the maximum mark of the exam?
Out of her total monthly income, a woman spends 60% on household expenses and 15% on savings. The remaining is spent on other items. What percentage of her income is spent on these other items?
The difference between 40% of a number and 25% of the same number is 75. What is 30% of the number?
A student got 33 1/3 % marks in an exam and failed by 20 marks. If he had got 50%, he would have passed by 10 marks. Find total marks.
A student's marks in Mathematics were 20% more than in Physics. If he scored 72 in Physics, how many marks did he score in Mathematics?
A man spends 80% of his income. His income has increased by 25%, but his expenditure remains the same. By what percent have his savings increased?
A reduction of 20% in the price of sugar enables a person to buy 5 kg more for Rs. 200. What is the original price per kg?
In a town, 20% of the population migrated to another city. Later, 25% of the remaining population left due to lack of jobs. If the final population is 48,000, what was the original population?
A student scored 75% marks in an exam of 600 marks. How many marks did the student obtain?
A man spends 30% of his salary on rent, 20% on groceries, and 25% on other expenses. If he saves Rs. 9,000, what is his salary?
In an election, candidate A got 45% of the votes and candidate B got 30%. What percentage of votes did others get?
If a product price is decreased by 12% and then increased by 15%, what is the net percentage change in price?
The population of a village increased from 20,000 to 23,000 in a year. What is the percentage increase?
The price of petrol rises by 8%. If the old price was Rs80 per litre, what is the new price?
A student's marks rise from 400 to 450. What is the percentage increase?
A man has a salary of Rs1000 and spends Rs200. What percentage of his salary is spent?
A number is increased by 20% and then decreased by 20%. What is the net change?
A worker's salary rises from Rs12,000 to Rs13,200. What is the percentage increase?
The price of rice rises from Rs40 per kg to Rs50 per kg. What is the percentage increase?
A student scores 140 marks out of 200. What percentage of marks did he score?
A person has an income of Rs 400 and spends Rs 300. What percentage of income is saved?
In an office of 1000 people, 400 are women. What percentage are women?
In a village of 2000 people, 800 are women. What percentage of the population is women?
The price of an item decreases from 500 to 425. What is the percentage decrease?
A worker's wage increases from Rs. 200 to Rs. 240. What is the percentage rise?
Person A earns 25% more than Person B. What percentage less does B earn compared to A?
A candidate scores 270 marks out of 300. What percentage of marks did he obtain?
A student's marks increase from 70 to 84, What is the percentage increase?
A man earns Rs500 and saves Rs100. What percentage of his income is saved?
If 30% of a number x equals 180, find x.
A student answers 18 questions correctly out of 20. What is the accuracy percentage?
A man earns Rs1000 and saves Rs200. What percentage of income is spent?
In a company of 600 workers, 40% are women. How many women are there?
The price of a book rises from 200 to Rs220. What is the percentage increase?
A student scores 25 out of 50 in an exam. What percentage of marks did he secure?
In a village with 500 people, 300 are females. What percentage of the population is female?
A village has 10,000 people. The population increases by 10% in the first year and 20% in the second year. What is the population after two years?
An employee earns Rs6000 and spends 4800. What percentage of income is saved?
Out of 100 items, 25 are defective. What percentage is defective?
The price of a cycle falls from Rs3000 to Rs2700. What is the percentage decrease?
The value of an item falls from to . What is the percentage decrease?
The population of a town is 20,000. It increased by 10%. What is the new population?
A man has Rs 600 and spends Rs 450. What percentage of his total money is spent?
An amount of Rs600 increases to Rs660. What is the percentage increase?
The price of sugar increases by 25%. By what percentage should a family reduce its consumption so that the expenditure remains the same?
A wildlife reserve has 200 deer. Out of them, were tagged with GPS collars for tracking. How many deer were tagged?

Aastha spent 34% of his monthly income on entertainment, 22% on food, 18% on studies, 12% on clothing and rest on travelling. Difference between the amount spent on entertainment and travelling is Rs. 3,600. Find the amount spent on food.
In a half yearly exam, 50% of total candidates failed in physics, 40% of candidates failed in chemistry and 10% in both. Find the percentage of those who passed in both the sections.
A lab technician measured a solution as 198 ml instead of the actual 200 ml. What is the percentage error in measurement?
An island had 10,000 birds. Due to habitat loss, the bird population decreased by 10% one year, and then increased by 15% the next year after conservation efforts. What is the final population?
A provision store sells four-fifth of its fresh eatables daily. If the store gets 450 eatables daily, how many of them are sold?

In an election contested by two candidates, 1,780 votes were declared invalid. The winning candidate secured 82% of the valid votes and triumphed by a margin of 80 votes. Determine the total number of votes cast in the election.

Two candidates appeared for an examination. The marks obtained by one candidate exceeded those of the other by 75. Moreover, his score was equivalent to 80% of the combined marks of both candidates. Determine the marks obtained by each candidate.
In an election, there were only two candidates. One of the candidates secured 40% of the votes and was defeated by the other candidate by 4,65,104 votes. What is the total number of votes polled?
In an election, there were only two candidates. One of the candidates secured 30% of the votes and was defeated by the other candidate by 3,65,104 votes. What is the total number of votes polled?
30 percent of a number exceeds 20 percent of the same number by 40. What is the value of the number?

Every day a person sells 40 percent of his fruits and 10 percent of the remaining fruits get spoilt during the night. If total 36 fruits got spoilt on the first night, then how many fruits did he sell on the first day?
A student was supposed to multiply a number by 19/18 but instead multiplied it by 9/8. Find the percentage error in the student's calculation.
A man spends 83% of his income. If his income increases by 23% and his expenditure increases by 13%, find the percentage increase in his savings.
In a certain examination, 67% of the candidates successfully passed, while 165 candidates failed. Determine the total number of candidates who appeared for the examination.

A town has a population of 75,000, of which 50% are males. Among the males, 35% are literate. It is further known that 45% of the total population is literate. Determine the percentage of literate females in the town.

In an election between two candidates, one got 55% of the total valid votes. If 20% of the votes were invalid and the total number of votes was 9,000, what was the number of valid votes the other candidate got?
Instead of increasing a number by 25%, a student decreased that number by 25% and then increased the obtained number by 25%. By what percentage is the answer less than the original number?

The cost of a table exceeds that of a chair by Rs5,000. If the combined price of 16 tables and 14 chairs amounts to Rs2,00,000, determine by what percentage the price of a chair is less than the price of a table.
A fruit vendor disposed of 70% of his stock of apples. If, after this transaction, he is left with 720 apples, determine the initial quantity of apples he possessed.
A man's income increases by 21% and his expenditure increases by 9%. If his initial income was Rs45,000 and expenditure Rs27,000, find the percentage increase in savings.
The difference between the two numbers is 80% of the larger number. If the smaller number is 1500, find the larger number.
If x% of y equals 1500 and simultaneously y% of z equals 2000, establish the algebraic relationship connecting x and z.
50 percent of 30 percent of a number is equal to 150. What is the 40 percent of that number?
A man allocates 75% of his monthly income to expenditures and still manages to save Rs25,000 each month. Determine his monthly expenditure.

Every day a person sells 30 percent of his fruits and 20 percent of the remaining fruits get spoil during the night. If total 28 fruits got spoil on the first night, then how many fruits did he sell on the first day?
A person paid Rs. 30 as tax on an article worth Rs. 600. What is the rate of tax?
A vessel contains 36% water, 40% milk, and the rest is sugar. Determine the quantity of each component in a 1 kg sample.
18 out of total 3600 parts of earth is iron. What is the percentage of iron in earth?
A person spends 40 percent of his income. His income increases by 50 percent and his saving increases by 50 percent. What will be the percentage increase in the expenditure?
Which number is 50 percent less than 40 percent of 900?
20 out of total 2400 parts of earth is iron. What is the percentage of iron in earth?
The income of a person is Rs. 80000 and his expenditure is Rs. 45000. His income is increased by 15 percent and expenditure is increased by 20 percent. What will be the value of his new savings?
P's salary is firstly increased by 25 percent and then again increased by 40 percent. What is the overall percentage increase in P's salary?
25 percent of a number exceeds 15 percent of the same number by 50. What is the value of the number?
When 4 percent of a number is added to 11 percent of the same number, then the result is 1845. What is the value of the number?
The salary of a worker is firstly increased by 50 percent and thereafter reduced by 30 percent. What is the net change in the salary?
In an organisation, 50 percent employee are matriculates, 20 percent of remaining are graduates and now remaining 120 are post-graduates. What is the number of matriculates employee?
What percent of 0.05 cubic meters is 25,000 cubic centimeters?
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (Graduate), 02 Aug 2025, Shift 3
